A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 10 leads

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 18 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 14 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for Profund VC (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. ai_workflow_automation“Here is a European seed-stage AI-native workflow automation startup similar to Dust or Bardeen, targeting knowledge worker productivity in B2B context.”
2. regulated_intermediary_fintech“Here is a European seed-stage B2B SaaS startup automating KYC, AML, or regulatory reporting for insurance brokers or wealth managers under MiFID II or AIFMD.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.

Cambrion90MATCHThe company provides an AI-powered platform for extracting structured data from documents, which directly aligns with the 'ai_workflow_automation' angle for automating repetitive document processing for operations, finance, or HR teams. 🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 10 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 30 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 30 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod
THIS RUN — 2 seeds for Profund VC (test mode: 2 of 5 prod seeds):
1. Cognigy (anchor)
2. Shift Technology (anchor)
ⓘ No date filter — find_similar does not support freshness. Deduplication handles freshness. MATCHes become seeds in future runs.

It also aligns with the 'ai_workflow_automation' angle as it utilizes AI for workflow automation within the insurance sector.https://friss.com
Resistant AI - Fraud Detection90MATCHThe company provides B2B AI-powered document fraud detection for financial services, aligning with 'regulated_intermediary_fintech' by automating KYC/AML workflows for European financial institutions. They are headquartered in Prague, Czechia, satisfying the European requirement.https://resistant.ai
CLARA Analytics10NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed: The company provides AI solutions for commercial insurance, which does not align with 'early-stage B2B software product primarily sold to professional or institutional buyers' as outlined in the gate question's financial technology or AI workflow automation context for specific teams (operations, finance, legal, HR). Its focus is on claims processing for the commercial insurance industry, which is a niche and established domain, with no clear indication of 'early-stage' B2B software aligned with AI workflow automation for specific teams or regulated intermediary fintech. Its customer base lies outside the specified 'operations, finance, legal, or HR teams at European SMEs or mid-market companies'.https://claraanalytics.com
Corsa30NO MATCHSTEP 1 (regulated_intermediary_fintech) fails because Corsa is focused on fintechs and digital asset teams globally, not specifically European regulated intermediaries under EU frameworks, and is headquartered in the US with presence in Israel, not Europe-based for the specific matching criteria.https://corsa.finance
Oscilar40NO MATCHOscilar does not align with 'regulated_intermediary_fintech' because it is not focused on the European market, nor does it align with 'ai_workflow_automation' due to its lack of European focus and its primary target being larger financial institutions, not necessarily SMEs or mid-market companies as defined in the thesis.https://oscilar.com
🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — Profund VC
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with after:yesterday filter — always the last 24h. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the after:yesterday filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 150
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 7 companies → evaluated: 3 for Profund VC

LinkedIn Jobs — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (Profund VC)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 22 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads

The company develops an animation platform for game and animation studios, which is not aligned with the 'operations, finance, legal, or HR teams' target for 'ai_workflow_automation' nor the 'European regulated intermediaries' for 'regulated_intermediary_fintech'.https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/nura-studios-emerg
Online Oceans30NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed because the company's core business involves hardware (autonomous marine vessels) and data sales, not a pure B2B software product.https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/online-oceans-rais
Jupid0NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed for both [ai_workflow_automation] and [regulated_intermediary_fintech] angles as the company is based in the United States, not Europe.https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/jupid-raises-840k-
Axoft10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (CORE business) fails. Axoft is a neurotechnology company developing implantable brain-computer interfaces, which is a hardware-focused biotech/medical device company, not a software product (SaaS, AI workflow, or regulated-intermediary technology).https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/axoft-raises-55m-i
Techcyte10NO MATCHThe company operates in digital pathology diagnostics, which does not align with the 'ai_workflow_automation' angle focused on operations, finance, legal, or HR teams in European SMEs, nor the 'regulated_intermediary_fintech' angle. Additionally, the company is based in the US, failing the 'Headquartered outside Europe' reject criterion for 'ai_workflow_automation'.https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/techcyte-raises-15
Ctruh20NO MATCHThe company's core business of building XR/AI infrastructure for the spatial internet and 3D experiences (retail, e-commerce, D2C, real estate, automotive, education, healthcare) does not align with the specified AI-native workflow automation for operations, finance, legal, or HR teams, nor with B2B SaaS for European regulated intermediaries under specific financial frameworks. This fails STEP 1.https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/ctruh-raises-2-5m-
Parallel Web Systems20NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails - the company description focuses on building a 'Programmatic Web specifically for AIs' and a 'new open web' for AI users, which is too general and foundational to fit as an early-stage B2B software product for specific professional buyers or regulated intermediaries, nor does it align with defined AI workflow automation or regulated intermediary fintech. The focus is on a new web infrastructure for AI rather than a B2B SaaS product.https://parallel.ai/about
Pursuit10NO MATCHSTEP A failed. The company's description indicates it is a program that trains individuals and places them in tech roles, not a B2B software product.https://www.pursuit.org/our-team
BioticsAI10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails because BioticsAI focuses on early fetal abnormality detection, which is a healthcare application, not directly related to "AI-native workflow automation" for enterprise teams or "regulated intermediary fintech." It does not fit the B2B SaaS for operations, finance, legal, or HR teams, nor does it fit the regulated intermediaries in finance or insurance. Even if it uses AI, the core domain is outside the thesis.https://profiles.startuphealth.com/company-profile
Firestorm Labs0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because Firestorm Labs builds aerial vehicle systems, which is a hardware product, not a B2B software product.https://sossecinc.com/company/firestorm-labs/
Skio10NO MATCHINSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — not enough info on company activity to evaluate STEP 1.https://skio.com/team
Shapes10NO MATCHSTEP 1 of the GATE QUESTION requires a B2B software product; Shapes is described as 'an app to hang out in groupchats with AI and friends', which is clearly a consumer-facing application.https://f.inc/portfolio/shapes/
SpaceX backer 137 Ventures0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (CORE business) failed. 137 Ventures is an investment firm, not a B2B software product company.https://www.137ventures.com/
Faraday Future0NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed. Faraday Future manufactures electric vehicles, which is a hardware business, not B2B software as required by the gate question.https://www.ff.com/us/our-company/

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run
THIS RUN — 1 entry for Profund VC:
Week 18 → angle: ai_workflow_automationParis | Productivity Tools | 1-10 employees
ⓘ operatingStatus=Active · companyType=For Profit · Country auto-filled from city.

ai_workflow_automation weight=4 — 7 entries
in 16d — Mon May 18Monday:Paris | Artificial Intelligence | 1-10 empl.
in 17d — Tue May 19Tuesday:London | Machine Learning | 11-50 empl.
in 18d — Wed May 20Wednesday:Berlin | Artificial Intelligence | 1-10 empl.

in 20d — Fri May 22Friday:Stockholm | Machine Learning | 11-50 empl.
in 21d — Sat May 23Saturday:Paris | Productivity Tools | 1-10 empl.
in 22d — Sun May 24Sunday:London | Artificial Intelligence | 11-50 empl.
regulated_intermediary_fintech weight=3 ACTIVE NOW — 7 entries
in 2d — Mon May 4Monday:Paris | Insurance | 1-10 empl.
in 3d — Tue May 5Tuesday:London | Compliance | 11-50 empl.
in 4d — Wed May 6Wednesday:Berlin | Insurance | 1-10 empl.

in 6d — Fri May 8Friday:Paris | Compliance | 11-50 empl.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).
Backfill — VC Portfolios & Directories (Shared)
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 121 leads

Firecrawl + Gemini 2.5 Flash → scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages and extracts portfolio companies. Rotates through all sources (~9/day). Gemini reads the raw HTML and returns a JSON list of {name, url, description}.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.
Pappers FR — Company Registry (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

French company registry (INPI via Pappers API) → finds recently registered companies by NAF code. 90-day lookback by default. Only covers French companies — useful if your thesis includes French-founded B2B SaaS.

Shared | Rotation: NONE — all NAF codes daily (90-day lookback) | Cost: Pappers subscription

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.

clawnify90MATCHThe company develops an 'n8n for agents,' described as a 'declarative, AI-native workflow format that agents can read, write, and run,' which directly aligns with the 'ai_workflow_automation' angle of building an AI-native workflow automation product for business use, allowing agents to automate tasks.https://github.com/clawnify/clawflow
clawnify10NO MATCHThe company describes itself as 'The n8n for agents. A declarative, AI-native workflow format that agents can read, write, and run.' This is a generic AI workflow tool, not specifically focused on European corporate travel technology, nor does it align with any of the specified active angles for hotel booking, TMC workflow, corporate travel payments, or travel ERP integrations. It fails STEP A from the GATE QUESTION due to lack of travel domain focus.https://github.com/clawnify/clawflow
cisco-open10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because the company (Cisco) is a large, established technology firm, specifically not an early-stage B2B software product company as required by the gate question.https://github.com/cisco-open/ai-harness-toolkit
cisco-open0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (CORE business) fails. The company develops AI-native engineering workflows and agent skills for software development, which is unrelated to corporate travel technology. There is no evidence of hotel booking, travel policy, payments, TMC workflow, or ERP integration for business travel.https://github.com/cisco-open/ai-harness-toolkit

juliosuas10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (CORE business) fails as the company's offering 'AI Garden' is a consumer-facing, generative art project, not a B2B software product.https://juliosuas.github.io/ai-garden/
juliosuas0NO MATCHThe company 'juliosuas' operates an AI-powered pixel-art world focused on generative art and collaborative experiments, which is purely a consumer/leisure offering and has no connection to B2B corporate travel technology in Europe. It fails STEP A of the gate question.https://juliosuas.github.io/ai-garden/

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.

CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.

sforbt
4 MATCH · 93 NO MATCH · 4% hit rate
🌿 Evergreen Multi-Engine Sourcing System
Improve any engine without redeployment:
• Airtable: exa_queries, competitor_anchors, sectors, naf_codes
• engine_config.json: RSS feeds, Google News templates, caps
• backfill_engine.py: VC portfolio URLs
• New MATCHes auto-seeded into Exa Similar for next cycle.

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 10 leads

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 18 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 28 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for sforbt (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. corporate_travel_payments“Here is a European corporate travel payment platform similar to AirPlus or Conferma Pay, targeting finance directors needing automated hotel payment and VAT recovery.”
2. travel_erp_integrations“Here is a European B2B SaaS company building ERP integration middleware connecting corporate travel booking systems to SAP or Oracle for automated GL coding and invoice posting.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.
QUERY POOL — Full Rotation Cycle (28-day full cycle · test: 1/day per thesis · 28 total):
hotel_booking_saas weight=5 — 7 entries
in 18d — Wed May 20“Here is a European B2B SaaS platform automating corporate hotel booking and preferred hotel program management for enterprise travel managers, replacing manual GDS searches.”
in 19d — Thu May 21“Here is a hotel booking technology sold to Procurement Officers and CFOs in European multinationals seeking centralized lodging spend visibility and policy compliance.”
in 20d — Fri May 22“Here is a French or German startup providing a corporate hotel marketplace with negotiated rates and automated booking for mid-market firms with significant travel budgets.”
in 21d — Sat May 23“Here is a Here is an early-stage European SaaS company at Seed or Series A building hotel sourcing and RFP management for travel procurement teams in professional services firms.”
in 22d — Sun May 24“Here is a European travel tech startup integrating AI-powered hotel inventory aggregation with real-time policy enforcement and duty of care alerts for business travelers.”
in 23d — Mon May 25“Here is a European startup in corporate extended-stay or group lodging procurement, adjacent to transient hotel booking for enterprise clients with high-frequency travel.”
in 24d — Tue May 26“Here is a European corporate hotel booking platform similar to HRS Group or Roomex, targeting mid-market and enterprise buyers needing multi-property hotel consolidation.”
tmc_workflow_platform weight=4 — 7 entries
in 25d — Wed May 27“Here is a European SaaS platform automating TMC mid-office workflow including ticketing, queue management, and booking servicing for travel management companies.”
in 26d — Thu May 28“Here is a workflow automation tool sold to TMC operations directors and travel agency team leads in Europe to reduce manual agent workload per transaction.”
in 27d — Fri May 29“Here is a French or UK startup providing back-office automation software for travel agencies handling corporate accounts and multi-GDS booking queues.”
★ TODAY“Here is a Seed or Series A European company building mid-office automation that enables small TMCs to compete with Amex GBT and CWT on servicing costs.”
in 1d — Sun May 3“Here is a European startup using robotic process automation or AI to handle GDS scripting, PNR management, and exception handling for travel management companies.”
in 2d — Mon May 4“Here is a European startup adjacent to TMC workflow in corporate meetings management, group travel operations, or event travel logistics automation for enterprises.”
in 3d — Tue May 5“Here is a European TMC workflow automation platform similar to Travelport or KDS, targeting travel management companies needing agent productivity and booking servicing software.”
corporate_travel_payments weight=4 — 7 entries
in 4d — Wed May 6“Here is a European B2B SaaS or fintech platform automating corporate hotel invoice capture and cross-border VAT recovery for finance directors managing large travel budgets.”
in 5d — Thu May 7“Here is a virtual card issuance and lodging payment reconciliation tool sold to CFOs and treasury teams in European multinationals to eliminate manual hotel invoice processing.”
in 6d — Fri May 8“Here is a French or German startup providing AI-powered hotel folio extraction and EU VAT reclaim automation for corporate travel finance teams.”
in 7d — Sat May 9“Here is a Here is an early-stage European fintech company at Seed or Series A automating PCI-compliant hotel payment workflows integrated with SAP or Oracle ERP systems.”
in 8d — Sun May 10“Here is a European startup using machine learning for multi-currency hotel invoice reconciliation and automatic GL coding into enterprise accounting systems for travel spend.”
in 9d — Mon May 11“Here is a European company adjacent to travel payments in corporate card management, spend analytics for lodging, or procurement payment orchestration for business travel programs.”
in 10d — Tue May 12“Here is a European corporate travel payment platform similar to AirPlus or Conferma Pay, targeting finance directors needing automated hotel payment and VAT recovery.”
travel_erp_integrations weight=3 — 7 entries
in 11d — Wed May 13“Here is a European B2B SaaS company building ERP integration middleware connecting corporate travel booking systems to SAP or Oracle for automated GL coding and invoice posting.”
in 12d — Thu May 14“Here is a data integration platform sold to IT Directors and Finance Managers in European enterprises to eliminate manual re-keying between TMC back-office and accounting systems.”
in 13d — Fri May 15“Here is a French or German startup providing a travel-to-ERP data connector that automates hotel invoice coding and booking data synchronization for mid-market European companies.”
in 14d — Sat May 16“Here is a Seed-stage European SaaS startup building middleware to integrate hotel booking data with corporate finance systems and automate travel spend reconciliation workflows.”
in 15d — Sun May 17“Here is a European startup using API-first architecture to connect TMC back-office data feeds with enterprise ERP systems including multi-currency hotel invoice classification.”
in 16d — Mon May 18“Here is a European startup adjacent to travel ERP integration providing NDC content aggregation middleware, travel data warehousing, or booking analytics infrastructure for enterprises.”
in 17d — Tue May 19“Here is a European travel back-office integration platform similar to SIAP or Datalex, selling ERP connectors and data middleware to TMCs and corporate travel departments.”

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 10 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 60 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 60 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — sforbt
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with after:yesterday filter — always the last 24h. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the after:yesterday filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 150
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 7 companies → evaluated: 3 for sforbt
ALL 32 QUERIES — sforbt (all active every day):
QUERIES BY ANGLE (32 total · 0/day prod · 0/day test per thesis):
hotel_booking_saas weight=5 — 8 queries
1. “"corporate hotel booking" platform startup Europe Series A”
2. “"hotel sourcing" SaaS "travel manager" Europe funding”
3. “"online booking tool" hotel corporate Europe acquisition”
4. “"preferred hotel program" software startup B2B”
5. “"hotel aggregator" corporate travel Europe SaaS”
6. “"réservation hôtelière" entreprise logiciel startup France”
7. “"business hotel" booking platform TMC integration Europe”
8. “"hotel RFP" software procurement travel managers startup”
tmc_workflow_platform weight=4 — 8 queries
1. “"TMC software" OR "travel management company platform" startup Europe funding”
2. “"mid-office travel" automation SaaS Europe startup Series A”
3. “"travel agency" workflow automation B2B software Europe startup”
4. “"TMC" "back-office" SaaS startup Europe acquisition”
5. “"logiciel TMC" OR "agence voyages d'affaires" startup France SaaS”
6. “"travel servicing" automation platform Europe seed raise”
7. “TMC workflow automation Europe startup investment 2024 2025”
8. “"booking fulfillment" travel automation software B2B Europe”
corporate_travel_payments weight=4 — 8 queries
1. “"virtual card" hotel corporate travel Europe startup funding 2024 2025”

Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's google_alert_queries. Best formats: "[sector keyword] startup funding", "[company type] raises Series A", "[technology] B2B SaaS". Each query generates a Google News RSS URL with after:yesterday. Runs daily at no cost.
LinkedIn Posts — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ 0 posts this run

Apify LinkedIn Post Search → scans posts from the last 7 days (postedLimit=week, sortBy=date). Gemini Flash content miner reads each post and extracts company names from the text. 1 query/day per thesis, rotating through all queries.

Per-thesis | Rotation: test=2/day (1/thesis), prod=2/day (1/thesis) cycling through 16 queries | postedLimit: week | sortBy: date | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.10/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → each angle's apify_linkedin_posts_queries. Each phrase targets a different signal type. Best patterns: hiring signal (“hiring VP Sales [sector]”), funding signal (“raised seed [sector]”), product launch (“just launched [product type]”). 1 query runs per day per thesis — the full pool rotates over N days.
LinkedIn Jobs — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (sforbt)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 22 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads

Crunchbase — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.

Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.

Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. 🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.
CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.

[06:43:35]   [Exa Enrich] [1/20] Kibu | https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/kibu-raises-10-5m-in-seed-funding.html
